operating_state: Total revenue decreased $456 million to $25.4 billion driven by net interest income decline to $13.7 billion from higher deposit costs partially offset by higher asset yields. Noninterest income increased $636 million to $11.7 billion with investment banking fees and investment services revenue rising from higher asset management fees, offset by lower trading revenue from macro products. Global Banking net income decreased while Global Markets net income increased driven by higher sales and trading revenue.
cost_structure: Noninterest expense increased $271 million to $16.3 billion primarily from higher investments in people and revenue-related compensation, partially offset by lower litigation expense. Net interest yield decreased 13 basis points to 1.93 percent due to higher deposit costs, with efficiency ratio increasing to 64.26 percent. Provision for credit losses increased $383 million to $1.5 billion driven by higher credit card charge-offs and commercial real estate office portfolio losses.
material_events: The Board authorized a new $25 billion common stock repurchase program effective August 1, 2024 and declared a quarterly dividend of $0.26 per share, an eight percent increase. The Federal Reserve announced 2024 CCAR results with a stress capital buffer of 3.2 percent and CET1 minimum requirement of 10.7 percent effective October 1, 2024. Bank of America announced replacement of Three-Month CDOR with CORRA Fallback Rate for outstanding floating rate debt securities following CDOR cessation.
macro_exposures: The Corporation faces exposure to U.S. and global interest rates, inflation, currency exchange rates, and geopolitical instability including trade tensions. Commercial real estate office portfolio deterioration represents concentrated risk with $5.1 billion in reservable criticized exposure and $4.8 billion of office loans maturing by end of 2024. Interest rate sensitivity analysis shows asset sensitivity with $1.7 billion potential benefit from 100 basis point parallel rate increase under dynamic deposit assumptions.
forward_outlook: Management expects the new stress capital buffer of 3.2 percent effective October 1, 2024 through September 30, 2025 while continuing focus on expense management and revenue growth initiatives. The Corporation maintains disciplined capital allocation through the new share repurchase program and increased dividend payments, supported by $1.91 trillion in deposits.
uncertainty_profile: Commercial real estate office portfolio performance remains a key credit risk with stagnant demand and uncertain future space needs as companies evaluate remote versus conventional office employment models. Credit normalization continues with charge-offs expected to trend toward historical levels while regulatory capital requirements remain subject to implementation timing. The Corporation faces pending litigation and regulatory investigations across multiple areas.
